content.text: Indian food-delivery and quick-commerce platform — online restaurant aggregator and hyperlocal food delivery, plus Blinkit (quick commerce), District (going-out) and Hyperpure (B2B restaurant supplies).

thesis.heading: Competitive Moat
thesis.text: Zomato's defensibility is rooted in its leading market position within India's online food delivery and quick commerce sectors. It services over 300,000 restaurants and engages over 25 million monthly transacting users. The market's duopoly structure in food delivery, coupled with Blinkit's strong market share in quick commerce, creates significant barriers to entry for new competitors. Further diversification into B2B supplies (Hyperpure) enhances its ecosystem.

thesis.heading: Current Story
thesis.text: Zomato has demonstrated a significant financial turnaround, transitioning to profitability with a substantial increase in EBITDA from ₹42 crore in FY2024 to ₹637 crore in FY2025. This performance is underpinned by robust revenue growth, reaching ₹20,243 crore in FY2025, up from ₹12,114 crore in FY2024, indicating successful execution and operational leverage.

thesis.heading: Forward Opportunity
thesis.text: Zomato is well-positioned to capitalize on the high-growth Indian market. The India Online Food Delivery Market is projected at USD 45.15 billion in 2024 with a 22–23% CAGR through 2030, while the India Quick Commerce Market is estimated at USD 5.3 billion in 2025 with a 42.02% CAGR (2026-2034). Key catalysts include continued expansion into Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities and leveraging technology and AI for enhanced operational efficiency and customer experience.

data.Average Order Value (AOV): ₹568 in Q1 2023; 5% year-on-year (YoY) growth in Q4 FY24; INR 1,500 for its B2B supplies business (Hyperpure)
data.Monthly active users (MAU): 80 million as of March 2023; average monthly transacting user base for food delivery grew 8% year-on-year to 18.4 million in FY24
data.Average orders per MAU: Average monthly order frequency grew 7% year-on-year in Q4 FY24
data.Commission rates (restaurant/store side): Typically ranges from 15-25%, with rates often between 20-25% in metro cities; overall commission can potentially go as high as 45% with additional fees
data.Zomato Gold/Pro subscriber count: 3.8 million (38 lakh) members by November 2023; 7.4 million subscribers as of March 31, 2024

data.Delivery cost per order: Zomato incurred approximately INR 52 per delivery, while customers paid around INR 21 per delivery, as of June 2022.
data.Average delivery time: The average delivery time for Blinkit improved to 12.5 minutes in March 2024.
data.Rider churn rate: A report from June 2022 indicated rising attrition among Blinkit riders due to a shift from a salaried model to a per-order incentive model, but a specific churn rate was not provided.
data.Order fulfillment rate: Blinkit reported an order accuracy (fulfillment) rate of 99%+ in March 2024.
data.Dark store density/coverage (for Blinkit: As of March 2026, Blinkit operates over 2,000+ Micro-Fulfillment Centers (dark stores). In Q4 FY24, Blinkit's total store count was 526. Zomato aimed to reach 1,000 Blinkit stores by the end of FY25. As of Q4 FY26, the total store count for Blinkit was 2,243 stores.

full product list.name: Food Delivery
full product list.description: An online platform for customers to search, discover, order food, and have it delivered from local restaurants. This includes features like restaurant discovery, menus, user reviews, and recommendations.

full product list.name: Quick Commerce (Blinkit)
full product list.description: Rapid delivery (typically under 15 minutes) of products across various categories such as fresh produce, staples, electronics, beauty, and general merchandise.

full product list.name: Going-out (formerly Dining-out) & Ticketing (District)
full product list.description: This segment enables discovery and transactions for dining out, as well as ticketing for events, movies, concerts, and outdoor entertainment.

full product list.name: B2B Supplies (Hyperpure)
full product list.description: A business-to-business service that supplies quality food ingredients and other products directly to restaurants, sourcing from farmers and manufacturers.

B2C.description: Central to its food delivery and quick commerce (Blinkit) services, directly connecting urban dwellers (aged 18-45, including working professionals, students, and tech-savvy individuals) with restaurants and quick commerce stores. Primarily operates on a digital-first approach through its mobile application and website.

B2B.description: The Hyperpure division provides B2B supply chain solutions to the HoReCa (Hotel/Restaurant/Cafe) industry, offering kitchen supplies and supply chain management.

key customers if disclosed.details: Over 25 million monthly transacting users utilize the app for food discovery and ordering.
More than 500,000 delivery partners earn their livelihood through Zomato.

App store ratings & reviews.Google Play Store: 4.7 stars out of 5, based on 13 million reviews
App store ratings & reviews.Apple App Store: Generally positive reviews, with users describing the app as 'excellent and reliable,' 'very fast and no lag,' and offering 'reasonable price and good coverage.' Some negative feedback mentions 'fraud,' 'products missing expired food,' 'horrible customer service,' and 'too much spam notifications.'
App store ratings & reviews.Period: Current as of June 2026, with individual reviews dating back several years

Feature release cadence.Methodology: Agile methodology, specifically Scrum, involving 'time-boxed sprint cycles' and 'incremental software development.'
Feature release cadence.Frequency: Continuous and iterative release process driven by feedback; precise sprint duration not explicitly stated.

User interface (UI)/user experience (UX).App Framework Design: Designed for an 'excellent user interface experience,' featuring an 'intuitive design and visual appeal together with convenient navigation' and a 'minimalistic and usable design.'

AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Personalized Recommendations: AI algorithms analyze user history, location, time, and preferences to suggest restaurants and dishes, leading to a 35% increase in app engagement and a 22% increase in orders per user per month.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Logistics Optimization: Real-time AI routes riders, predicts traffic and delays using historical data, and optimizes rider allocation for over 500,000 delivery partners, reducing delivery times to 10-15 minutes in key areas.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Delivery Time Prediction (ETA): AI models analyze historical delivery data and real-time traffic conditions for accurate ETAs, continuously improving accuracy.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Customer Support: AI agents handle queries, refunds, and escalations, resolving up to 70% of issues without human intervention.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Menu Management: AI scans and optimizes restaurant menus using OCR and analyzes food images for better recommendations.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Dynamic Pricing and Demand Forecasting: AI optimizes pricing and promotions based on demand, user behavior, and competitor pricing, helping to maximize revenue during peak times.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Fraud Detection: AI is used to detect fraudulent activities like fake reviews and orders.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Generative AI: Utilized for hyper-personalized campaigns, synthetic content, and ad creatives.
AI/ML integration in logistics/personali.Impact: AI investments have boosted user retention to 70-80% and cut operational costs by 20-30%.

Tech talent acquisition/retention metric.Employee Satisfaction: Glassdoor ratings indicate 'decent overall employee satisfaction levels.'
Tech talent acquisition/retention metric.Retention Strategies: Continuous training and development, employee well-being policies (strong parental leave and health benefits), an open-door policy, and encouragement of innovation.
Tech talent acquisition/retention metric.Recruitment Strategy: CEO Deepinder Goyal has stated a preference against hiring active job seekers, emphasizing building a strong employer brand and investing in employee development. Entry-level recruitment includes a written examination, a technical interview, and a final HR interview.

data.revenue split by region with % (and grow: Not Available. A recent, explicit revenue split by region with percentages and growth is not available in Zomato's latest annual reports or investor presentations for FY23 or FY24. Older data from May 2016 indicated India and the UAE contributed 45% and 20% of Zomato's revenues respectively, but this information is outdated. The 'Going-out' segment, which includes operations in both India and the UAE, reported a revenue of INR 258 crore in FY24, marking a 51% year-on-year growth from INR 171 crore in FY23; however, a further split of this revenue between India and the UAE is not disclosed.

United Arab Emirates (UAE): HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) Certification
Basic Food Safety Training and Person in Charge Certification

data.regional revenue trend: Zomato's overall revenue has shown strong growth, primarily driven by its operations in India. In FY24 (year ended March 31, 2024), total revenue was INR 12,995 crore (up 66.5% from FY23), operating revenue was INR 12,114 crore (up 71.1% from FY23), and adjusted revenue was INR 13,545 crore (up 56% year-on-year from INR 8,693 crore in FY23). The company achieved profitability for the first time in FY24, reporting a net profit of INR 351 crore, a significant turnaround from a loss of INR 971 crore in FY23. This growth is attributed to the strong performance across its key business segments (food delivery, quick commerce, going-out services, and B2B supplies), all of which are predominantly India-centric.

positive triggers: Strong Market Positioning and Diversified Offerings: Zomato holds a leading position in the Indian food delivery market and has successfully diversified its business into quick commerce (Blinkit), dining out (District), and B2B supplies (Hyperpure).
Growth in Quick Commerce (Blinkit): Blinkit, Zomato's quick commerce arm, is demonstrating significant growth in gross order value and is anticipated to be a key driver of execution variance.
Return to Profitability: Zomato achieved a net profit of ₹351 crore in FY2024, a notable turnaround from a net loss of ₹971 crore in FY2023.
Improving Unit Economics: The core food delivery business is showing improving contribution margins and stable cash flows.
Robust Balance Sheet: Recent capital raises have strengthened Zomato's financial position.
Ambitious Expansion Plans: The company aims to expand its Blinkit store network significantly, targeting 1,000 stores by March 2025 and 2,000 by December 2025.

Innovation and Enhanced Customer Experience: Zomato has introduced new initiatives such as "veg only" and "large order" fleets, along with "delivery on priority," to improve customer experience and explore new monetization avenues.
negative triggers: Intense Competition: The food delivery sector operates as a duopoly, and the quick commerce segment also faces significant competitive pressures.
High Valuation: Zomato's stock is considered to be trading at a high valuation (419x P/E), implying that substantial future growth is already factored into its current price.
Profitability Pressure from New Investments: Increased investments in Blinkit's infrastructure and higher employee expenses have led to a decline in consolidated Adjusted EBITDA in Q4 FY25.
Thin Margins and Credit Risks in Hyperpure: The Hyperpure B2B supply business operates with thin margins and carries inherent credit risks.
Slowdown in Discretionary Spending: A broader economic slowdown impacting discretionary consumer spending could negatively affect Zomato's business.

4-7 risk flags with severity (red/amber/.description: Intense Competition and Market Share Volatility. The food delivery and quick commerce markets are highly competitive, with a duopoly structure in food delivery. This can lead to aggressive pricing, marketing wars, and potential erosion of market share or profitability.

High Valuation and Investor Expectations. The stock's high P/E ratio (419x) indicates that significant future growth is already priced in, making it vulnerable to any shortfall in performance or changes in investor sentiment.

Profitability Impact from New Business Investments. While strategic, heavy investments in expanding quick commerce (Blinkit) and other new ventures can initially dilute overall profitability and adjusted EBITDA margins.

Customer Dissatisfaction and Brand Reputation. Reports indicate escalating consumer dissatisfaction concerning refund failures, delivery inefficiencies, opaque pricing, food safety, and app usability, which can harm brand reputation and customer loyalty.
Thin Margins and Credit Risks in B2B Segment (Hyperpure). The Hyperpure business, while growing, operates with inherently thin margins and is exposed to credit risks from restaurant partners.

Regulatory and Policy Changes. Changes in government regulations or policies related to online food delivery, quick commerce, or gig economy workers could impact operational costs and business models.
Economic Slowdown and Discretionary Spending. A general economic downturn or a significant reduction in consumer discretionary spending could lead to lower order volumes and average order values.

corporate milestones.description: Zomato was founded by Deepinder Goyal and Pankaj Chaddah.
The company had expanded its operations to over 100 cities across 18 countries.
Zomato acquired several international companies, including MenuMania (New Zealand), Lunchtime (Czech Republic), Obedovat (Slovakia), and Gastronauci (Poland).
Zomato acquired Urbanspoon.
Zomato invested in Indian local delivery specialists Pickingo and Grab.

Zomato made its stock market debut, becoming the first Indian unicorn to go public. Its shares listed at ₹115 on the BSE and ₹116 on the NSE, representing a 51% and 53% premium, respectively, over the issue price of ₹76. The company's market capitalization surpassed ₹1 lakh crore on its listing day.
Zomato reported its first-ever quarterly consolidated net profit of ₹2 crore.
The company reported a consolidated net profit of ₹175 crore.
Zomato became the first Indian startup to be included in the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) Sensex 30 index, replacing JSW Steel Limited.

Zomato's net profit surged by 346% year-on-year to ₹174 crore, primarily driven by the rapid growth of its quick commerce unit, Blinkit. Total revenue for the quarter increased by 196% year-on-year to ₹17,292 crore.

litigation status.status: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) ruled in favor of Zomato, dismissing an insolvency plea filed by B2B apparel manufacturer Nona Lifestyle over alleged unpaid dues of ₹1.64 crore (April 3, 2025). Zomato denied the allegations, citing a pre-existing contractual dispute.

The Delhi High Court issued a notice to the Competition Commission of India (CCI) and Zomato on a plea filed by the National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI) challenging its exclusion from a 'confidentiality ring' in an ongoing antitrust probe (April 21, 2025). The NRAI accused Zomato and Swiggy of anti-competitive conduct, including mandating their delivery services, concealing customer data, imposing high commissions, and favoring their own cloud kitchens.

The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission in Dharwad, Karnataka, ordered Zomato to pay ₹50,000 as compensation for mental stress and ₹10,000 for legal expenses to a customer who did not receive a momos order placed in August 2023 (July 15, 2024).

ESG initiatives: Inclusion in FTSE4Good Index Series in January 2025.

Unveiled sustainability goals for 2030 in January 2024, aiming for net-zero emissions across its food delivery value chain by 2033, supporting 300,000 restaurant businesses, empowering 1 million gig workers, and providing 300 million nutritious meals to underprivileged communities.
Aims to reduce last-mile delivery emissions by 70% on a per km basis by FY30 from a base year of FY22, with a commitment to facilitate 100% EV-based deliveries by 2030.
Implemented 'Climate Aware Delivery' program requiring customers to opt-in for plastic cutlery, aiming to reduce plastic waste by up to 2 million kilograms annually.
Launched 'Low Plastic Packaging' filter in December 2024 to help users find restaurants using sustainable alternatives.
Runs a voluntary program to recycle plastic waste, having recycled approximately 45,000 MT of plastic waste since FY23.
Established the Feeding Foundation, which provided over 65 million meals during COVID-19 and aims to alleviate hunger.
Implements energy conservation initiatives like 'Turn It Off' and prioritizes LED lighting in corporate offices.
Promotes reusable cutlery and a 'Bring Your Own Bottle (BYOB)' program in offices, along with waste segregation training for employees.
